page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-48 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 4 NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 90 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon CPU with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.2 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance split between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng, gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the average range (middle 50%). Conversely, the server excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier (top 10%) performance in prompt processing for 135M, 7B, and 70B models, and strong to top-tier results in text generation.

The hardware profile represents a specialized resource density tradeoff. While the CPU architecture delivers lower single-threaded efficiency, the integration of four dedicated L4 GPUs makes it highly cost-efficient for parallel computing workloads rather than general-purpose CPU tasks. The lack of local storage requires external storage solutions, which should be factored into the overall architecture design.

This server configuration is designed for GPU-accelerated workloads such as large language model (LLM) inference, machine learning model deployment, and parallel graphics processing. It is less suitable for single-threaded CPU tasks, basic web serving, or high-I/O local database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-48 is a graphics-optimized server equipped with 4 NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering 90 GB of total VRAM. The host system features 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (24 physical cores) operating at 2.2 GHz and 192.0 GB of system memory, with no local storage included. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant divergence: single-core CPU capabilities are weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. However, the server achieves top-tier results in LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models. This hardware profile is highly efficient for parallelized GPU workloads, machine learning inference, and graphics rendering, though it presents a tradeoff for applications reliant on high single-threaded CPU performance or local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-48 is a graphics-optimized server with 4 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 192 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference but weaker single-core CPU execution.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-48 features 4 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 192 GB RAM, optimized for LLM inference and parallel computing.
tagline: A quad-GPU accelerated server featuring Ada Lovelace architecture and 48 dedicated vCPUs, optimized for high-throughput language model inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with 4 NVIDIA L4 GPUs providing 90 GB of total VRAM
Features 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for LLM inference and prompt processing
Exhibits lower single-core CPU performance, ranking in the bottom 25%
Lacks local storage, requiring external storage configurations
Suited for machine learning deployment, parallel graphics, and AI inference
